title: ONGC Physics — Full-length Mock 04
pattern: ONGC CBT — 85 MCQ · 120 min · 1 mark each · no negative marking

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier series of f(x) = x on the interval (−π, π) contains only:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="360" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="180" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Fourier Series of f(x)=x</text><line x1="50" y1="100" x2="310" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="180" y1="30" x2="180" y2="180" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="60" y1="170" x2="180" y2="40" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="180" y1="40" x2="300" y2="170" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><text x="155" y="185" font-size="11" fill="#475569">−π</text><text x="175" y="185" font-size="11" fill="#475569">0</text><text x="288" y="185" font-size="11" fill="#475569">π</text><text x="50" y="95" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">y</text><text x="305" y="95" font-size="11" fill="#64748b">x</text><text x="245" y="75" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444">sin terms only</text></svg>

options: A constant term plus cosine terms
questions.solution: f(x) = x is an odd function on (−π, π). For odd functions, all cosine coefficients aₙ = 0. Only sine coefficients are nonzero: bₙ = (2/n)(−1)^(n+1). The Fourier series is f(x) = 2[sin x − sin 2x/2 + sin 3x/3 − ···].

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A 2×2 real symmetric matrix A has trace 5 and determinant 6. The sum of its eigenvalues is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 160" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="340" height="160" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="170" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Matrix Eigenvalue Properties</text><text x="170" y="50" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">tr(A) = λ₁ + λ₂ = 5</text><text x="170" y="75" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#1e293b">det(A) = λ₁ × λ₂ = 6</text><text x="170" y="110" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">Characteristic eq: λ² − 5λ + 6 = 0</text><text x="170" y="135" font-size="12" text-anchor="middle" fill="#3b82f6">→ λ₁ = 2, λ₂ = 3</text></svg>

questions.solution: For any square matrix, the sum of eigenvalues equals the trace: λ₁ + λ₂ = tr(A) = 5. The product equals the determinant: λ₁ · λ₂ = det(A) = 6. We can verify: λ₁, λ₂ are roots of λ² − 5λ + 6 = 0 → (λ−2)(λ−3) = 0 → λ₁ = 2, λ₂ = 3.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Legendre polynomial P₂(cos θ) is equal to:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="340" height="170" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="170" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Legendre Polynomials P₀, P₁, P₂</text><line x1="50" y1="100" x2="290" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="170" y1="35" x2="170" y2="160" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><text x="295" y="95" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">x</text><text x="175" y="42" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">y</text><line x1="50" y1="80" x2="290" y2="80" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/><text x="295" y="78" font-size="9" fill="#22c55e">P₀=1</text><line x1="50" y1="120" x2="290" y2="80" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><text x="295" y="100" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6">P₁=x</text><path d="M50,110 Q100,65 170,45 Q240,65 290,110" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/><text x="295" y="125" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444">P₂=(3x²−1)/2</text></svg>

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Fourier transform of the Dirac delta function δ(t) is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 170" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="340" height="170" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="170" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">δ(t) ↔ 1  (Fourier Transform Pair)</text><line x1="95" y1="40" x2="95" y2="155" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="93" y1="155" x2="93" y2="40" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5"/><polygon points="93,40 88,52 98,52" fill="#3b82f6"/><text x="105" y="50" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6">∞</text><text x="170" y="105" font-size="16" fill="#64748b">⟺</text><line x1="255" y1="75" x2="310" y2="75" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><text x="270" y="70" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444">= 1</text><text x="95" y="170" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">t</text><text x="255" y="170" font-size="10" fill="#64748b">ω</text></svg>

questions.solution: F(ω) = ∫₋∞^∞ δ(t)e^(−iωt)dt = e^(−iω·0) = 1. The Fourier transform of δ(t) is the constant 1 for all ω — a delta function in time corresponds to all frequencies equally.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A particle moves in an inverse-square force field F = −k/r². The orbit is circular when the angular momentum equals:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 340 180" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="340" height="180" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="170" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Central Force Orbits</text><circle cx="90" cy="100" r="50" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/><circle cx="90" cy="100" r="3" fill="#ef4444"/><text x="90" y="165" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">Circle</text><ellipse cx="250" cy="100" rx="70" ry="40" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" fill="none"/><circle cx="195" cy="100" r="3" fill="#ef4444"/><text x="250" y="165" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#475569">Ellipse</text><text x="170" y="30" font-size="10" text-anchor="middle" fill="#64748b">L = √(mkr₀) for circular orbit of radius r₀</text></svg>

questions.solution: For a circular orbit of radius r₀: mv²/r₀ = k/r₀² → v² = k/(mr₀). Then L = mvr₀ = m√(k/(mr₀))·r₀ = √(mk r₀). This gives the unique angular momentum for a circular orbit.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Two identical oscillators of mass m are connected by three identical springs of constant k (wall–m–k–m–wall). The symmetric mode frequency is:

figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 360 140" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="360" height="140" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="180" y="20" font-size="13" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold" fill="#1e293b">Two Coupled Oscillators</text><line x1="30" y1="70" x2="80" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/><rect x="80" y="58" width="24" height="24" fill="#3b82f6" rx="3"/><text x="92" y="75" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">m</text><line x1="104" y1="70" x2="146" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><rect x="146" y="58" width="24" height="24" fill="#ef4444" rx="3"/><text x="158" y="75" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="white">m</text><line x1="170" y1="70" x2="210" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5" stroke-dasharray="3,2"/><line x1="210" y1="70" x2="260" y2="70" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="125" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#22c55e">k</text><text x="158" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#f59e0b">k</text><text x="195" y="58" font-size="9" text-anchor="middle" fill="#22c55e">k</text><text x="180" y="130" font-size="11" text-anchor="middle" fill="#64748b">ω_sym = √(k/m)</text></svg>

questions.solution: For the symmetric mode, both masses move in the same direction. The middle spring doesn't stretch. Each mass feels only its wall spring k. Equation of motion: mẍ = −kx, so ω_sym = √(k/m). The antisymmetric mode has ω = √(3k/m).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A damped harmonic oscillator has mass m, damping b, spring constant k. The condition for critical damping is:
questions.solution: Characteristic equation: ms² + bs + k = 0, giving s = (−b ± √(b²−4mk))/(2m). Critical damping: discriminant = 0 → b² − 4mk = 0 → b = 2√(mk).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A uniform solid cylinder of mass M and radius R rolls without slipping down an inclined plane. Its acceleration is:
questions.solution: For rolling without slipping: Ma = Mg sin θ − f, Iα = fR, a = Rα. For a solid cylinder I = ½MR². So f = ½Ma. Then Ma = Mg sin θ − ½Ma → a = (2/3)g sin θ.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A particle moves under a central force F = F(r)r̂. Which quantities are conserved?
questions.solution: For a central force F = F(r)r̂, torque τ = r × F = 0 (r parallel to F). So angular momentum L is conserved. The force is conservative, so total energy E is also conserved. Linear momentum is NOT conserved (there is an external force).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A parallel-plate capacitor has area A, plate separation d, filled with dielectric of permittivity ε. The capacitance is:
questions.solution: C = εA/d where ε = εᵣε₀ for a dielectric with relative permittivity εᵣ. The dielectric increases capacitance by factor εᵣ compared to vacuum.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The polarization P of a dielectric material is related to the electric field E by:
questions.solution: P = ε₀χₑE, where χₑ is the electric susceptibility. D = ε₀E + P = ε₀(1 + χₑ)E = εE where ε = ε₀εᵣ and εᵣ = 1 + χₑ.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A solenoid with N turns, length L carries current I with a core of permeability μ. The magnetic field inside is:
questions.solution: For an ideal solenoid with core permeability μ: B = μnI = μ(N/L)I = μNI/L.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In free space, the displacement current density is:
questions.solution: The displacement current density is J_D = ε₀∂E/∂t. Maxwell added this to Ampère's law to ensure consistency with charge conservation: ∂ρ/∂t + ∇·J = 0.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The energy levels of a one-dimensional quantum harmonic oscillator are:
questions.solution: Eₙ = (n + ½)ℏω for n = 0, 1, 2, .... The ½ℏω zero-point energy is purely quantum — the particle cannot be at rest due to the uncertainty principle.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A particle of energy E encounters a rectangular barrier V₀ (width a) with E < V₀. The transmission coefficient T:
options: Is always zero for E < V₀
Decreases exponentially with barrier width a
questions.solution: For E < V₀: T ≈ (16E(V₀−E)/V₀²)·e^(−2κa) where κ = √(2m(V₀−E))/ℏ. Key feature: exponential decrease with width a.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Quantum Mechanics · Hydrogen Atom Fine Structure
questions.stem: The fine structure splitting of the hydrogen atom n = 2 level gives levels with total angular momentum j =:
questions.solution: For n = 2: 2S₁/₂ has j = 1/2; 2P states split into 2P₁/₂ (j=1/2) and 2P₃/₂ (j=3/2). The Lamb shift further splits 2S₁/₂ and 2P₁/₂.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Heisenberg uncertainty principle states:
questions.solution: The Robertson-Schrödinger relation gives Δx · Δp ≥ ℏ/2. Equality holds for Gaussian wave packets — minimum uncertainty states.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The wave function of a particle must be:
questions.solution: Physical wave functions must be single-valued, continuous, and square-integrable. They need not be real — complex wave functions are common.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: From the Helmholtz free energy F = U − TS, which Maxwell relation follows?
questions.solution: From dF = −S dT − P dV, since F is a state function: d²F/(dTdV) = d²F/(dVdT), giving (∂S/∂V)_T = (∂P/∂T)_V.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Clausius-Clapeyron equation gives dP/dT for a phase boundary. For liquid-gas equilibrium, dP/dT:
options: Depends on the sign of ΔV
questions.solution: Clausius-Clapeyron: dP/dT = L/(TΔV). For liquid→gas ΔV > 0, so dP/dT > 0. For solid→liquid (most substances) ΔV < 0, giving dP/dT < 0 (water is the exception).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Near a second-order phase transition, M ∝ (T_c − T)^β. The critical exponent β for the 2D Ising model is:
questions.solution: The 2D Ising model (Onsager solution) gives β = 1/8 exactly. Mean-field theory predicts β = 1/2, incorrect in low dimensions.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Boltzmann's entropy S = k_B ln W. For W = 10²³, the value of S/k_B is:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In LS coupling, the ground state term symbol of carbon (Z=6, configuration 1s²2s²2p²) is:
questions.solution: For p²: Hund's rules give (1) maximum S → S=1 (triplet), (2) maximum L → L=1 (P), (3) less-than-half-filled: J = |L−S| = 0. Ground state is ³P₀.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The rotational constant B of a diatomic molecule is related to the moment of inertia I by:
questions.solution: The rotational constant is B = ℏ²/(2I), where I = μr². Energy levels: E_J = BJ(J+1) = ℏ²J(J+1)/(2I).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In Raman spectroscopy, the ratio of anti-Stokes to Stokes intensity at temperature T is:
questions.solution: I_anti-Stokes/I_Stokes = exp(−ℏω/k_BT). Anti-Stokes requires initial excited state (Boltzmann factor), so weaker at room temperature.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The He-Ne laser operates at a wavelength of:
questions.solution: He-Ne laser emits at 632.8 nm (visible red). 10.6 μm is CO₂, 532 nm is Nd:YAG, 337 nm is N₂ laser.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The Franck-Condon factor |⟨v'|v''⟩|² is largest when:
questions.solution: Franck-Condon factor is largest when equilibrium internuclear distances are similar — vertical transition lands near v'=0 maximum.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a 1D monatomic lattice, the group velocity of phonons at the Brillouin zone boundary (k = π/a) is:
questions.solution: ω = 2√(C/M)|sin(ka/2)|. v_g = dω/dk = a√(C/M)|cos(ka/2)|. At k = π/a: v_g = 0. Standing wave.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In BCS theory, Cooper pair formation is mediated by:
questions.solution: In BCS theory, two electrons form a Cooper pair by exchanging virtual phonons. One electron polarizes the lattice, attracting the second electron.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Below T_c in a ferromagnet, spontaneous magnetization arises from:
questions.solution: Below T_c, the quantum mechanical exchange interaction aligns neighboring electron spins parallel, creating magnetic domains.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In the tight-binding model, the bandwidth W of a 1D chain with hopping integral t is:
questions.solution: E(k) = E₀ − 2t cos(ka). Bandwidth W = (E₀+2t) − (E₀−2t) = 4t.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In intrinsic silicon at room temperature, nᵢ ≈:
questions.solution: nᵢ for Si at 300 K is 1.5 × 10¹⁰ cm⁻³. Smaller band gap → higher nᵢ.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The current through a PN junction under forward bias V is given by:
questions.solution: Shockley equation: I = I₀[exp(qV/nk_BT) − 1], where I₀ is reverse saturation current, n is ideality factor (1-2).

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In a BJT common-emitter configuration, I_C = β I_B. The current gain β typically ranges from:
questions.solution: β = h_FE is the DC current gain, typically 50–300 for small-signal transistors. Also: I_E = I_C + I_B = (β+1)I_B.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: An RC low-pass filter has cutoff f_c = 1/(2πRC). At f = f_c, the output voltage is:

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: In ¹⁴₇N + ⁴₂He → ¹⁷₈O + X, the particle X is:
questions.solution: A conservation: 14+4=17+A → A=1. Z conservation: 7+2=8+Z → Z=1. X = proton. Rutherford's 1919 experiment.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: Which quantity is always conserved in nuclear reactions?
questions.solution: Baryon number is conserved in all interactions. Parity is violated in weak interactions. Strangeness is conserved in strong but not weak.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: The quark content of a proton is:
questions.solution: Proton = uud. Charge: 2(2/3) + (−1/3) = +1. Neutron = udd: 2/3 + 2(−1/3) = 0.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.stem: A radioactive substance has half-life 5 years. What fraction remains after 20 years?
questions.solution: Half-lives = 20/5 = 4. Fraction = (½)⁴ = 1/16.

questions.subject: Domain Knowledge
questions.topic: Nuclear & Particle Physics · Nuclear Binding Energy
questions.stem: ⁵⁶Fe has B/A ≈ 8.8 MeV while ²³⁵U has B/A ≈ 7.6 MeV. This implies:
options: Fission of uranium and fusion of light elements both release energy
questions.solution: ⁵⁶Fe has highest B/A. Moving away (fission of heavier or fusion of lighter) releases energy. Uranium fission → fragments with higher B/A.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A product's price increases by 15% and then decreases by 10%. The net change is:

figure.caption: Pie chart of percentage distribution
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Percentage Breakdown</text><circle cx="160" cy="110" r="65" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M160,110 L160,45 A65,65 0 0,1 218,75 Z" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M160,110 L218,75 A65,65 0 0,1 218,155 Z" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M160,110 L218,155 A65,65 0 0,1 102,155 Z" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="185" y="70" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">35%</text><text x="210" y="120" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#ef4444" font-weight="bold">25%</text><text x="120" y="135" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#22c55e" font-weight="bold">40%</text><text x="370" y="110" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">Parts of 100%</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A can do a work in 20 days, B in 30 days. A works alone 5 days, then both work together. How many more days?
questions.solution: A's 5 days = 5/20 = 1/4 done. Remaining = 3/4. Combined rate = 1/20+1/30 = 1/12. Days = (3/4)/(1/12) = 9.

figure.caption: Work rates: combined effort reduces time
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Work Rate Diagram</text><rect x="80" y="55" width="160" height="30" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><rect x="80" y="100" width="120" height="30" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#22c55e" rx="4"/><rect x="80" y="145" width="200" height="30" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="4"/><text x="160" y="75" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">A: 1/6 per day</text><text x="140" y="120" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">B: 1/8 per day</text><text x="180" y="165" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle">Combined: 1/6+1/8 = 7/24</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A man buys for Rs.400 and sells at 20% loss. To gain 15%, he should sell at:
questions.solution: To gain 15%: SP = 400 × 1.15 = Rs.460. The 20% loss info is a distractor.

figure.caption: Cost vs selling price comparison
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Profit & Loss</text><rect x="80" y="70" width="70" height="70" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/><rect x="200" y="50" width="70" height="90" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/><rect x="320" y="90" width="70" height="50" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/><text x="115" y="110" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">CP</text><text x="235" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">SP > CP</text><text x="235" y="125" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle">Profit</text><text x="355" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">SP < CP</text><text x="355" y="130" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">Loss</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Average weight of 20 students is 60 kg. Including the teacher, average becomes 61 kg. Teacher's weight:

figure.caption: Average as horizontal line through data
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Average</text><line x1="60" y1="170" x2="440" y2="170" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="60" y1="30" x2="60" y2="170" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="80" y="120" width="30" height="50" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="2"/><rect x="120" y="80" width="30" height="90" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#22c55e" rx="2"/><rect x="160" y="100" width="30" height="70" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="2"/><rect x="200" y="60" width="30" height="110" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#ef4444" rx="2"/><rect x="240" y="90" width="30" height="80" fill="#8b5cf6" stroke="#8b5cf6" rx="2"/><line x1="60" y1="105" x2="280" y2="105" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5"/><text x="350" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Mean = Σxᵢ/n</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A bag has 5 red and 3 blue balls. Two drawn without replacement. P(both red) =

figure.caption: Venn diagram for probability union
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Probability Venn</text><circle cx="170" cy="110" r="55" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="300" cy="110" r="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/><text x="145" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">P(A)</text><text x="325" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" font-weight="bold">P(B)</text><text x="235" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">A∩B</text><text x="240" y="185" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">P(A∪B)=P(A)+P(B)−P(A∩B)</text></svg>

questions.stem: A:B = 3:5 and B:C = 2:3. Then A:B:C =

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A car goes from A to B at 40 km/h and returns at 60 km/h. Average speed is:

figure.caption: Distance-time graph

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Simple interest on Rs.5000 at 8% p.a. for 3 years is:

figure.caption: Compound interest grows faster than simple
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">SI vs CI Growth</text><line x1="60" y1="170" x2="440" y2="170" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="60" y1="30" x2="60" y2="170" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><path d="M80,150 L200,110 L320,70 L400,30" fill="none" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2.5"/><path d="M80,150 L160,120 L240,90 L320,60 L400,30" fill="none" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2" stroke-dasharray="5"/><text x="410" y="28" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">CI</text><text x="410" y="38" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444">SI</text><text x="240" y="195" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">CI > SI for same rate & time</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: How many 3-letter words can be formed from the letters of 'LOGARITHM' (no repetition)?
questions.solution: 9 letters available. 3-letter words = P(9,3) = 9×8×7 = 504.

figure.caption: Key difference: order matters vs not
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">P vs C</text><rect x="40" y="50" width="190" height="55" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><rect x="250" y="50" width="190" height="55" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" rx="4"/><text x="135" y="72" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Permutation</text><text x="135" y="90" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">ⁿPᵣ = n!/(n−r)!</text><text x="345" y="72" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Combination</text><text x="345" y="90" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">ⁿCᵣ = n!/r!(n−r)!</text><text x="240" y="140" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">ABC≠BAC (perm) | ABC=BCA (comb)</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: The LCM of 12, 15 and 20 is:

figure.caption: Nested number sets
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Number System Hierarchy</text><rect x="150" y="40" width="180" height="30" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><rect x="100" y="80" width="130" height="30" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" rx="4"/><rect x="250" y="80" width="130" height="30" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="4"/><rect x="120" y="120" width="90" height="30" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" rx="4"/><rect x="120" y="160" width="40" height="25" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#818cf8" rx="4"/><rect x="170" y="160" width="40" height="25" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#818cf8" rx="4"/><text x="240" y="60" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Real Numbers</text><text x="165" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Rational</text><text x="315" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Irrational</text><text x="165" y="140" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Integers</text><text x="140" y="177" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#818cf8" text-anchor="middle">ℕ</text><text x="190" y="177" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#818cf8" text-anchor="middle">0</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: A boat goes 24 km upstream in 6 hours and 24 km downstream in 3 hours. Speed of current =
questions.solution: Upstream = 24/6 = 4 km/h. Downstream = 24/3 = 8 km/h. Current = (8−4)/2 = 2 km/h.

figure.caption: Boat speed relative to stream current
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Boat in River</text><path d="M20,120 Q80,100 140,120 Q200,140 260,120 Q320,100 380,120 Q440,140 480,120" fill="none" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1.5"/><path d="M20,135 Q80,115 140,135 Q200,155 260,135 Q320,115 380,135 Q440,155 480,135" fill="none" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="1"/><rect x="190" y="100" width="50" height="25" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="8"/><text x="215" y="117" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Boat</text><text x="120" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e">Down: v_b+v_s</text><text x="350" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444">Up: v_b−v_s</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pipe A fills a tank in 12 hours, Pipe B empties it in 18 hours. Both open, tank fills in:
questions.solution: A rate = 1/12, B rate = −1/18. Net = 1/12−1/18 = 1/36. Time = 36 hours.

figure.caption: Cistern with inlet/outlet
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Pipe System</text><rect x="120" y="60" width="120" height="100" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2" rx="4"/><line x1="245" y1="90" x2="310" y2="90" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2.5"/><line x1="245" y1="130" x2="310" y2="130" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="180" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">Cistern</text><text x="277" y="83" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle">Pipe A (fill) →</text><text x="277" y="145" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">Pipe B (empty) →</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Father is 30 years older than son. In 5 years, father's age will be twice son's age. Present age of son =
questions.solution: Let son = x, father = x+30. In 5 years: x+35 = 2(x+5) → x+35 = 2x+10 → x = 25.

figure.caption: Age relationship at different time points
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Age Timeline</text><line x1="60" y1="100" x2="440" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="120" cy="100" r="4" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6"/><circle cx="240" cy="100" r="4" fill="#22c55e" stroke="#22c55e"/><circle cx="360" cy="100" r="4" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#ef4444"/><text x="120" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">Now</text><text x="240" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle">+5 yrs</text><text x="360" y="85" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">+10 yrs</text><text x="240" y="140" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">Father: x  |  Son: x−25</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In what ratio must rice at Rs.30/kg be mixed with rice at Rs.45/kg to get a mixture worth Rs.40/kg?

figure.caption: Alligation cross method
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Alligation Cross</text><circle cx="100" cy="80" r="35" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="380" cy="80" r="35" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="240" cy="130" r="30" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="135" y1="80" x2="210" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/><line x1="345" y1="80" x2="270" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1.5"/><text x="100" y="84" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">Cheaper</text><text x="380" y="84" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">Dearer</text><text x="240" y="134" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle">Mean</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Two trains of lengths 150 m and 200 m run in opposite directions at 54 km/h and 72 km/h. Crossing time =
questions.solution: Relative speed = 54+72 = 126 km/h = 35 m/s. Distance = 350 m. Time = 350/35 = 10 sec.

figure.caption: Train and platform crossing
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Train Crossing Platform</text><rect x="40" y="80" width="180" height="30" fill="#3b82f6" stroke="#3b82f6" rx="4"/><line x1="40" y1="125" x2="440" y2="125" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="2"/><rect x="250" y="80" width="180" height="30" fill="#f59e0b" stroke="#f59e0b" rx="4"/><text x="130" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Train (L)</text><text x="340" y="100" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#fff" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Platform (P)</text><text x="240" y="170" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle">Time = (L+P)/speed</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: What comes next: 3, 8, 18, 38, ?
questions.solution: Differences: 5, 10, 20, 40 (doubling). Next = 38+40 = 78.

figure.caption: Series with increasing differences
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Number Series</text><line x1="60" y1="100" x2="420" y2="100" stroke="#94a3b8"/><circle cx="100" cy="100" r="20" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6"/><circle cx="180" cy="100" r="20" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e"/><circle cx="260" cy="100" r="20" fill="#fef3c7" stroke="#f59e0b"/><circle cx="340" cy="100" r="20" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444"/><circle cx="420" cy="100" r="20" fill="#e0e7ff" stroke="#818cf8"/><text x="100" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">2</text><text x="180" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle">6</text><text x="260" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle">12</text><text x="340" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">20</text><text x="420" y="105" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#818cf8" text-anchor="middle">?</text><text x="240" y="160" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">+4, +6, +8, +10 → 30</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If SKILL is coded as TKHLM, then CODE is coded as:
questions.solution: Each letter shifted +1: C→D, O→P, D→E, E→F = DPFE.

figure.caption: Letter-to-letter coding pattern
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Letter Code</text><text x="160" y="70" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#3b82f6" font-weight="bold">A  B  C  D  E  F  G</text><text x="160" y="90" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#f59e0b">↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓  ↓</text><text x="160" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#ef4444" font-weight="bold">Z  Y  X  W  V  U  T</text><text x="240" y="160" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">Reverse cipher: A→Z, B→Y, ...</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Pointing to a man, a woman says 'He is the son of my mother's only daughter.' How is the man related to the woman?
questions.solution: My mother's only daughter = myself. He is my son.

figure.caption: Family tree for blood relation problems
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Family Tree</text><circle cx="160" cy="50" r="16" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="320" cy="50" r="16" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="176" y1="50" x2="304" y2="50" stroke="#f59e0b" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="240" y1="50" x2="240" y2="85" stroke="#64748b" stroke-width="1.5"/><circle cx="160" cy="120" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="240" cy="120" r="14" fill="#fee2e2" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="320" cy="120" r="14" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="160" y2="106"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="240" y2="106"/><line x1="240" y1="85" x2="320" y2="106"/><text x="160" y="54" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">M</text><text x="320" y="54" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">F</text><text x="160" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">S</text><text x="240" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle">D</text><text x="320" y="124" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle">S</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Rahul walks 10 km North, turns right and walks 5 km, turns right and walks 10 km. He is now:

figure.caption: Cardinal directions compass
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Compass Rose</text><circle cx="240" cy="110" r="65" fill="#f0f9ff" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="240" y1="45" x2="240" y2="175" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><line x1="175" y1="110" x2="305" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="1"/><path d="M240,110 L270,70" fill="none" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="2.5"/><text x="240" y="42" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#ef4444" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">N</text><text x="240" y="185" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#3b82f6" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">S</text><text x="315" y="114" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#22c55e" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">E</text><text x="165" y="114" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">W</text><text x="280" y="65" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#ef4444">NE</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Statements: All dogs are animals. Some animals are cats. Conclusions: I. Some dogs are cats. II. Some cats are animals.
questions.solution: From 'Some animals are cats' by conversion: 'Some cats are animals' (II follows). 'Some dogs are cats' does not follow.

figure.caption: Venn diagram for syllogism
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Syllogism Venn</text><circle cx="180" cy="110" r="50" fill="#dbeafe" stroke="#3b82f6" stroke-width="2"/><circle cx="300" cy="110" r="50" fill="#dcfce7" stroke="#22c55e" stroke-width="2"/><text x="165" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#3b82f6">All A</text><text x="315" y="80" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#22c55e">Some B</text><text x="240" y="115" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="9" fill="#f59e0b" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Overlap?</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If P > Q >= R < S, which is definitely true?
questions.solution: From P > Q >= R, we get P > R by transitivity.

figure.caption: Inequality on number line
figure.markup: <svg viewBox="0 0 480 200" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><rect width="480" height="200" fill="#f8fafc" rx="8"/><text x="240" y="20" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="12" fill="#4f46e5" text-anchor="middle" font-weight="bold">Number Line</text><line x1="60" y1="110" x2="420" y2="110" stroke="#94a3b8" stroke-width="2"/><line x1="120" y1="100" x2="120" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8"/><line x1="200" y1="100" x2="200" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8"/><line x1="300" y1="100" x2="300" y2="120" stroke="#94a3b8"/><line x1="200" y1="95" x2="420" y2="95" stroke="#ef4444" stroke-width="3"/><circle cx="200" cy="95" r="5" fill="#ef4444" stroke="#ef4444"/><text x="120" y="135" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">0</text><text x="200" y="135" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">5</text><text x="300" y="135" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="10" fill="#64748b" text-anchor="middle">10</text><text x="310" y="88" font-family="sans-serif" font-size="11" fill="#ef4444" font-weight="bold">x > 5</text></svg>

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: Four friends P, Q, R, S sit around a square table. P is left of Q, R is opposite P. Who is right of R?
questions.solution: Clockwise: Q, P, S, R. S is right of R.

figure.caption: Circular arrangement with positions

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: In a class of 45, Meena's rank from top is 18. Rank from bottom =
questions.solution: Rank from bottom = 45 - 18 + 1 = 28.

questions.subject: Aptitude
questions.stem: If January 1, 2024 is Monday, what day is January 1, 2025?
questions.solution: 2024 is a leap year (366 days). 366 mod 7 = 2. Monday + 2 = Wednesday.

questions.stem: The area of a circle with radius 7 cm is (use pi = 22/7):

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which organization is the nodal agency for petroleum and natural gas in India?
options: Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas
questions.solution: The Ministry of Petroleum and Natural Gas (MoPNG) is the government ministry responsible for oil and gas policy.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC was founded in which year?
questions.solution: ONGC was established on August 14, 1956, as a government company.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Bombay High offshore oil field is located in which basin?
questions.solution: Mumbai High is India's largest offshore oil field, located about 160 km west of Mumbai.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which Indian satellite series is used for communication and broadcasting?
questions.solution: Both INSAT and GSAT series are used for communication, broadcasting, and meteorology.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Paris Agreement on climate change aims to limit warming to:
questions.solution: Paris Agreement (2015): well below 2°C, with efforts to limit to 1.5°C.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: ONGC's headquarters is located in:
questions.solution: ONGC is headquartered in Dehradun, Uttarakhand.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: Which organization manages India's Strategic Petroleum Reserves?
questions.solution: ISPRL manages India's strategic petroleum reserves at Visakhapatnam, Mangalore, and Padur.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's first nuclear power station was established at:
questions.solution: Tarapur Atomic Power Station (TAPS) in Maharashtra, commissioned in 1969.

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: India's largest oil-producing state is:
questions.solution: Rajasthan has been India's largest oil-producing state since 2009 (Barmer district).

questions.subject: General Awareness
questions.stem: The Richter scale measures:
questions.solution: The Richter scale measures earthquake magnitude based on seismic wave amplitude.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most similar in meaning to 'ABUNDANT':
questions.solution: Abundant = existing in large quantities; plentiful.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most opposite in meaning to 'BENIGN':
questions.solution: Benign = gentle, harmless. Malignant = harmful, hostile.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Identify the grammatically correct sentence:
options: Neither the teacher nor the students was present.
Each of the boys have completed their assignment.
The committee has decided to approve the proposal.
She don't know the answer to the question.
questions.solution: 'The committee has decided' — collective noun takes singular verb. Others: (A) 'were', (B) 'has', (D) 'doesn't'.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: What does the idiom 'to burn the midnight oil' mean?
options: To work late into the night
questions.solution: Means to work or study late into the night.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: A person who loves books is called a:
questions.solution: Bibliophile = book lover. Philatelist = stamp collector. Numismatist = coin collector.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct preposition: 'The meeting is _____ Monday.'
questions.solution: We use 'on' with days of the week.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Passage: 'The monsoon is a seasonal wind pattern that brings heavy rainfall to the Indian subcontinent. It is driven by the differential heating of land and sea.' What primarily drives the monsoon?
options: Differential heating of land and sea
questions.solution: The passage explicitly states it is driven by differential heating of land and sea.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the correct form: 'By the time we arrived, the train _____.'
questions.solution: Past perfect 'had left' for action completed before another past action.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: Choose the word most similar in meaning to 'ELOQUENT':
questions.solution: Eloquent means fluent, persuasive, and expressive.

questions.subject: English Language
questions.stem: She has been working here _____ 2019.
questions.solution: 'Since' with a specific point in time (2019). 'For' with duration.
